title: Audio Mix Basics in Fairlight

video.note: Covers the Fairlight page, timeline continuity between pages, tracks, mute/solo, mixer, meters, dialogue tracks, clip attributes, selecting audio regions, ambience, music, track naming, track colors, sound effects, buses, effects, EQ, compression, and doing audio in passes.

learningTarget: I can organize audio tracks and make a basic sound pass that balances dialogue, music, ambience, and effects.

bellRinger.prompt: What makes video audio feel messy or hard to understand?

vocabulary.definition: The Resolve page designed for audio editing, mixing, and sound design.
The panel used to adjust track volume, pan, EQ, effects, and routing.
A visual display showing audio level during playback.
A control that turns a track off.
A control that plays one track while temporarily muting others.
Background room tone or environmental sound that makes a scene feel present.
A routing path that groups tracks into a shared mix or effects chain.
Equalization, used to adjust frequency ranges in sound.

slides.title: Lesson 06 - Audio Mix Basics in Fairlight

assignment.title: Dialogue, Music, and Sound Pass
evidenceRequired: Google Drive link to a screenshot of organized Fairlight tracks or mixer.
Google Drive or YouTube link to an exported audio-improved rough cut if teacher requests export.
Short reflection explaining one audio problem and how you fixed it.

exitTicket: Which audio layer mattered most in your mix today: dialogue, music, ambience, or sound effects?
